normalized_definitions.material_identifier: The unique name, designation, or identification code of the conditioned material under investigation.

normalized_definitions.manufacturing_process_description: A descriptive summary of the overall preparation, manufacturing, and processing steps applied to the base material prior to specimen extraction.

normalized_definitions.heat_treatment_parameters: Details describing the applied thermal treatments, including temperatures, durations, and cooling methods used to condition the material microstructure.

normalized_definitions.material_product_form: The physical shape or manufacturing state of the base bulk material (e.g., casting, hot-rolled bar, wrought assembly) from which test samples were extracted.

normalized_definitions.material_section_thickness: The specific thickness or diameter of the bulk material during processing and heat treatment, which dictates cooling rates, microstructural evolution, and subsequent localized corrosion properties.

normalized_definitions.specimen_geometry_and_dimensions: The precise physical shape, configuration, and exact dimensions of the prepared test electrode (e.g., flat coupon size, rod diameter) that influence exposure area and edge effects.

normalized_definitions.surface_preparation_and_finish: The comprehensive mechanical and chemical procedures applied to the specimen surface prior to testing. This includes abrasive grinding, polishing, chemical etching, pickling, and passivation techniques to remove cold-worked layers, as well as the resulting surface roughness, which critically influences localized pitting initiation.

normalized_definitions.exposed_surface_area: The exact geometric surface area of the prepared specimen that is actively exposed to the test electrolyte, typically measured and recorded in square centimeters.

normalized_definitions.specimen_orientation: The specific physical, manufacturing, or crystallographic plane of the specimen exposed to the test environment, accounting for anisotropic corrosion behavior across different planes.

normalized_definitions.crevice_former_specification: The material, geometry, and design of any physical device (e.g., TFE-fluorocarbon washers) utilized to artificially induce and localize crevice corrosion on the test specimen.

normalized_definitions.crevice_prevention_technique: The masking material, coating, or boundary technique (e.g., clear Glyptal varnish) applied to the specimen edges or mounts to inhibit unintended crevice corrosion that could artificially lower pitting potential measurements.

normalized_definitions.oxide_film_thickness: The measured or estimated thickness of any deliberately grown, anodized, or naturally occurring protective oxide film on the sample surface prior to testing, typically reported in micrometers.

normalized_definitions.welding_parameters: The specifics of any welding procedures applied to the material prior to testing, including the weld type (e.g., autogenous) and subsequent post-weld thermal or mechanical states.
